sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.39 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: A class of groups${ K}$is said to be radical\/ if it is closed under taking homomorphic images and normal subgroups and if every group generated by its normal${ K}$-sub\-groups also belongs to${ K}$. The question proposed relates to the topic ``Radical classes and formulae of Narrow (first order) Predicate Calculus (NPC)''. One can show that the only non-trivial radical class definable by universa...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
